front: The architect needs to clarify a detail without changing cost or time. Which document should be used?
The owner and contractor agree to modify the contract sum and time. Which document should be used?
The architect directs work to proceed before cost is finalized. Which document should be used?
The contractor needs clarification about conflicting drawings. Which document should be used?
The contractor proposes a different product than specified. Which document should be submitted?
The contractor submits fabrication details for review. Which document is this?
The architect documents observations during a site visit. Which document should be produced?
The architect confirms the building can be used for its intended purpose. Which document is issued?
The contractor requests payment for completed work. Which document is used?
The architect approves payment to the contractor. Which document is issued?
The contractor breaks down the contract sum into work categories. Which document is used?
The architect lists items that must be corrected before final completion. Which document is used?
The contractor records actual installed conditions for the project. Which document is produced?
The contractor provides equipment maintenance instructions. Which document is submitted?
The contractor provides guarantee coverage for installed materials. Which document is submitted?
The architect records decisions from a construction meeting. Which document should be produced?
